title: Step Increment Loop in While Loops

content.problemStatement:
A **Step increment loop** with a while loop involves repeatedly executing a block of code, each time increasing a counter variable by a fixed amount until a specific stopping condition is met. This method is useful for scenarios such as counting up, progressing through sequences in ascending order, and managing repetitive tasks with increasing control.

**Syntax:**

```java
int i = initial_value;       // Initial value
while (i < limit) {          // Condition to continue the loop
    // Loop body: Code to execute on each iteration

    i += step_increment;     // Increment step
}
```

---
**Example:**

```java
int i = 1;                   // Start from 1
while (i <= 10) {            // Continue as long as i is less than or equal to 10
    System.out.println(i);   // Print the current value of i
    i += 2;                  // Increment i by 2 each time (1, 3, 5, 7, 9)
}

```
**Expected Output:**
```default
1
3
5
7
9
```
